title: Stash Internals, Partial Work, and Portable Patches · Graph Lens: Gita Stash Apply Pop Branch
summary: An urgent repair interrupts mixed staged, unstaged, untracked, and ignored experiments; some work must remain visible, some move across clones, and no secret may enter an object. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita stash apply pop branch increment in work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose apply, pop, branch, store, create, export, or import from collision risk, portability, and retention needs.
Use pathspec, patch, staged, keep-index, include-untracked, and all controls while previewing secret and ignored-file consequences.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.

steps.title: Predict Gita Stash Apply Pop Branch
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G86 is a theory in a disposable repository. only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible. The learner must predict the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths, run git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip, explain policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection, test this changed condition: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes, and preserve this recovery boundary: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash.

# Worked focus: gita-stash-apply-pop-branch — Choose apply, pop, branch, store, create, export, or import from collision risk, portability, and retention needs.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5

git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip
# expected: the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths
# observation: policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection
# changed-case: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5

# recovery: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash
